one_time_description: Purchase premium on acquisition of Mafeking Services Ltd.
economic_notes: First Nation operates Gas Bar, Mafeking Services Ltd., New Country Motel, Video Lottery Terminals, and TLE Settlement fund. Investments in Aseneskak Casino Limited Partnership (14.29%), Mahihkan Bus Lines Limited Partnership (16.67%), and Nekote Limited Partnership (14.29%) accounted for by modified equity method. Acquired Mafeking Services Ltd. during the year.
notes: Province is Manitoba (MB), not AB. Consolidated entity includes 14 entities/departments including Mafeking Services Ltd. acquired during the year and 10162438 Manitoba Ltd. (Brandon Bus Depot). Six partnerships accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs). Restricted cash total is $310,354 (Ottawa Trust Fund $19,820 + New School $4,580 + TLE Settlement $285,954). Net debt reported as negative $9,222,884 at year end. Purchase premium on Mafeking Services Ltd. acquisition of $388,139 recorded as one-time expense. Adjustment to accumulated surplus of $324,905 related to Mafeking Services Ltd. acquisition. Long-term debt balance not extractable from provided text. TCA net book value not extractable from provided text. Adopted new PSAB standards PS 3400 Revenue and PS 3160 Public Private Partnerships effective April 1, 2023. ISC funding calculated as ISC general $9,564,950 + ISC Health $6,747,191 = $16,312,141. Total government transfers includes ISC, ESDC, CMHC, Province of Manitoba = $16,764,262. OSR = total revenue $23,860,531 minus total government transfers $16,764,262 = $7,096,269.
